ingredient_spotlight.label: Ingredient spotlight
ingredient_spotlight.body: It's in every serum, every toner, every moisturizer — but do you actually know why? Niacinamide (Vitamin B3) works across multiple biological targets simultaneously, making it every formulator's dream ingredient. Here's the chemistry behind the hype.

tiles.name: Melanogenesis
tiles.desc: Inhibits melanosome transfer between melanocytes and keratinocytes

tiles.name: Ceramide boost
tiles.desc: Stimulates fatty acid and ceramide synthesis in the stratum corneum

tiles.name: NMF increase
tiles.desc: Boosts natural moisturizing factor retention in the epidermis

tiles.name: NAD⁺ pathway
tiles.desc: Precursor to NAD⁺ — coenzyme critical for cellular energy and repair

profile.bio_1:
**I didn't become obsessed with skincare because my skin was perfect, I became obsessed with it when nothing seemed to work anymore.**

I started **Sofisticated** as a creative outlet to document my journey with skincare, hair care, and beauty. Growing up in Honduras, I never thought much about my skin, until moving to the U.S. to study **Chemical Engineering at Purdue** and finding that the routine that had always worked for me suddenly didn't. 

Between acne, hair struggles, and learning that beauty is rarely one-size-fits-all, I became fascinated by finding what actually works.
profile.bio_2: Today, **Sofisticated** is a space where I share the things I'm learning, the products that earn a permanent spot on my shelf, and experiences that have helped me feel more confident in my own skin, in hopes that they might help someone else too.

about.role: Chemical Engineering · Purdue University · Cosmetic Formulation
about.quote: I started Sofisticated because beauty marketing is full of buzzwords and zero chemistry. I want to show you **what's actually in your products**, how they interact with your skin at a molecular level, and how to shop smarter — **because you deserve to understand what you're putting on your face.**

newsletter.title: Stay in the know
newsletter.subtitle: New posts on ingredients, formulations and cosmetic chemistry — straight to your inbox. No fluff, just real science.
